angle: What it's like building a product while working full-time
threads: Building kakisewa while working full-time is basically: 8am-6pm doing my actual job, then 9pm-midnight arguing with myself about database schemas. Currently cold emailing 8,471 Malaysian rental agents at 100/day. Did I have time to build this? No. Did I do it anyway? Unfortunately yes.

slides: What building a product while working full-time actually looks like
Your 'deep work' hours are 10pm on a Tuesday
You ship features in 90-minute windows between meetings
Every weekend is a negotiation between rest and roadmap
Currently cold emailing 8,471 rental agents. 100 a day. One by one.
It's not romantic. It's just stubbornness with a Notion doc.
But it's the only way I know how to find out if this is real
carousel.caption: kakisewa is getting built in the gaps. Sharing the whole thing as it happens.

angle: Day 1 — Building in public: zero paying customers
caption:
I built a SaaS and have zero paying customers.

Here is what week 1 actually looked like.

What are you building right now? Drop it in the comments.
threads_text:
I built a SaaS and have zero paying customers.

Here is what week 1 actually looked like: the launch nobody saw, the Stripe dashboard I kept refreshing, and what building while employed actually costs you.

What are you building right now?

slides.main_text: I built a SaaS and have **zero paying customers.**
slides.sub_text: Here is what week 1 actually looked like.
After 7 days live. Fully launched. Real people able to sign up.
slides.bubble: I kept refreshing Stripe expecting something to happen. Nothing happened.

slides.main_text: I built it in **evenings and weekends.**
slides.sub_text: While working full-time at a US startup in Malaysia.
slides.sub_text_2: No co-founder. No investors. No announcement.
slides.bubble: My colleagues think I go to bed early.

slides.sub_text: No Product Hunt. No X post. No fanfare.
slides.sub_text_2: Just pushed it live and waited to see if anyone noticed.
slides.bubble: The scariest part was not launching. It was nobody noticing.

slides.main_text: The gap between **it is ready** and anyone caring.
slides.sub_text: Nobody is waiting for your product. You have to go find them.
slides.bubble: Week 1 taught me: building is the easy part. Distribution is the job.

slides.main_text: Zero customers does not mean **zero progress.**
slides.sub_text: It means you are still early enough to get it right.
slides.follow_cta: Follow to know what happens next or save this for later.
slides.bubble: Month 2 starts tomorrow. Let's see if the emails start converting.

angle: What working at a US startup from Malaysia is actually like
caption: Everyone romanticises working at a US startup. Here's what it actually looks like from Malaysia.
threads_text: Working at a US startup from Malaysia sounds cool until you're on a 10pm Zoom call while your friends are at mamak. The pay is decent. The timezone is not. You're always half a day behind everyone who matters. Still beats a lot of alternatives though, not gonna lie.

slides.main_text: Working at a **US startup** from Malaysia sounds like the dream.
slides.sub_text: Here's what nobody tells you about it.
Average time difference between KL and US West Coast
slides.stat_label: behind San Francisco. Every single day.
slides.bubble: My most productive hours are when my boss is asleep. Make of that what you will.

slides.main_text: Your **mornings are free**. Your evenings are not.
slides.sub_text: All the real meetings happen after 9pm KL time.
slides.sub_text_2: You learn to eat dinner early and keep your laptop charged.
slides.bubble: I have perfected the art of looking alert on camera at 11pm.

slides.main_text: The **pay gap** is real and it works in your favour.
slides.sub_text: US salary, Malaysian cost of living. The math is genuinely good.
slides.sub_text_2: This is the one part that lives up to the hype.
slides.bubble: Not going to pretend this isn't the reason most of us do it.

slides.main_text: But you are always **half a day behind** everyone who matters.
slides.sub_text: Decisions get made while you sleep. You catch up in the morning.
slides.sub_text_2: You get used to it. Doesn't mean it stops being annoying.
slides.bubble: Nothing like waking up to 47 Slack messages and a decision that affects your whole week.

slides.main_text: So I started building **kakisewa.com** in the pockets of time I actually own.
slides.sub_text: 8,471 cold emails out to rental agents. 100 a day, automated.
slides.sub_text_2: Zero paying customers so far. But the mornings are mine.
slides.bubble: Running a cold email campaign while my day job sleeps. Very normal behaviour.

slides.main_text: The **timezone gap** is the tax. The free mornings are the opportunity.
slides.sub_text: Still figuring out if I'm playing it right.
slides.follow_cta: Follow to know what happens next.
slides.bubble: Ask me again once someone actually pays for kakisewa.
